questions.question: A bookshelf has $5$ shelves, and each shelf can hold at most $8$ books. If the bookshelf is currently empty, what is the maximum number of books that can be placed on it, given that $x$ represents the number of books on each shelf, and $x \leq 8$?

Tom has been saving money for a new bike and has $120. He wants to buy a bike that costs $180. If he saves $15 per week, what is the minimum number of weeks he needs to save, given that $x$ represents the number of weeks, and $15x + 120 \geq 180$?

A water tank can hold $2400$ gallons of water. If $10%$ of the tank is already filled, what is the maximum amount of water that can still be added, given that $x$ represents the amount of water in gallons, and $x + 0.1 \cdot 2400 \leq 2400$?

A bakery sells a total of $250$ loaves of bread per day. They sell a combination of whole wheat and white bread. If they sell $x$ loaves of whole wheat bread, and $x - 20 \leq 150$, what is the maximum number of loaves of whole wheat bread they can sell?

A group of friends want to go on a road trip that is at least $240$ miles long. If they have already driven $x$ miles, and $x + 60 \geq 240$, what is the minimum number of miles they still need to drive?

A person has been training for a marathon and can run at most $8$ miles per hour. If they want to run a distance of $20$ miles, what is the minimum time it will take them to finish the marathon, given that $x$ represents the time in hours, and $8x \geq 20$?

A store has a $10%$ discount on all items. If an item originally costs $x$ dollars, and the discount is $0.1x$, what is the maximum amount someone can save if they buy an item that costs at most $100, given that $0.1x \leq 10$?
A person is planning a trip and wants to spend at most $500$ dollars. If they have already spent $x$ dollars, and $x + 50 \leq 500$, what is the maximum amount they can still spend?
Graph the solution to the inequality $2x - 5 > 11$ on a number line, and write the solution in interval notation. Show all work and explain your answer.
Solve the inequality $x/4 + 2 > 5$, and graph the solution on a number line. Write the solution in interval notation and provide a brief explanation.

teacher_rules: Ensure students have a graphing calculator to visualize the solutions.
Remind students to check their work and provide explanations for their answers.
Encourage students to use different methods to solve the inequalities, such as graphing and algebraic manipulation.
